rag_rules: Fix type errors by ensuring named things in AGREE contracts are valid expressions with known types, tracing unresolved identifiers or invalid helpers on the same line.
Move implementation-level references from component type annexes to implementation annexes to avoid scope mismatches.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must use only visible identifiers and helpers declared in the same annex scope.
Validator diagnostics pointing to type mismatches should be resolved by correcting helper declarations or removing invalid clauses.
Implementation-level bindings in AGREE must refer to valid outputs or subcomponent paths in the implementation scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and must declare valid expressions with proper types.
Component type contracts should reference only visible ports or features, not subcomponent paths or outputs.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level references in component type annexes; move valid bindings to the implementation annex instead.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the model's configured dependencies.
irrelevant_topics: Component type annexes for implementation-level bindings
